context: Agent audits a Python Dockerfile for layer ordering, cache efficiency, and cache cleanup placement issues.

checklist.name: COPY ordering cache bust identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that 'COPY . /app' before 'RUN pip install -r requirements.txt' causes the pip install cache to be invalidated on every source code change, and proposes copying requirements.txt first.

checklist.name: Separate cache cleanup layer explained
checklist.description: Agent correctly explains that 'RUN rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*' in a separate RUN instruction creates a new layer and does not reduce the size of the layer where apt-get ran; the cleanup must be in the same RUN layer.

checklist.name: Split pip install identified
checklist.description: Agent identifies that running pip install twice (once for requirements.txt, once for gunicorn) creates unnecessary layers and recommends combining into one RUN or adding gunicorn to requirements.txt.

checklist.name: USER placement assessed correctly
checklist.description: Agent notes the USER directive is present (good) but placed after all COPY and RUN instructions, which is the correct position for a non-root runtime user.

checklist.name: Corrected code snippet provided
checklist.description: Agent provides at least one corrected code block combining apt-get update, install, and cache cleanup in a single RUN layer.
